findings.evidence: The dict-comprehension rewrite silently drops the ascending-shortage sort. BASE returned items sorted by (threshold - quantity) ascending; HEAD returns them in arbitrary insertion order. report.restock_priorities relies on list_low_stock delivering an ascending-sorted list so it can slice low[-n:] and reverse() to obtain worst offenders first. Without the sort, restock_priorities produces wrong results on any real (unsorted) input.

Tests were re-written to stub list_low_stock with a pre-sorted mock, so they no longer exercise the real inventory->report integration. The suite passes on HEAD not because the behaviour is correct, but because the tests no longer test the broken contract. The false-green masks the regression introduced in inventory.py.

The docstring no longer documents the sort order guarantee that was present in BASE. Callers who relied on the documented ascending-shortage ordering (e.g. report.py line 10-11 comment) are silently broken with no indication in the public API.
